column1: # mapping_set_description: "SSSOM mappings from DataCite Metadata Schema 4.6 to DCTERMS (Dublin Core Terms) and DCAT-AP. Companion file to datacite-schemaorg.sssom.tsv; covers elements with no adequate Schema.org equivalent."
#   - This file is a STUB. Full mappings will be developed in a dedicated DataCite-DCTERMS/DCAT-AP sprint.
#   - Priority rows captured here are dateType values omitted from the Schema.org mapping due to
#     domain violations or missing Schema.org equivalents.
#   - Subject IRIs for sub-elements use the same nested path convention as the Schema.org file.

column7: comment
DataCite's mandatory DOI maps directly to dcterms:identifier
Primary agents map to dcterms:creator
Primary title maps to dcterms:title
Publisher maps to dcterms:publisher
Publication year maps to dcterms:issued
Subject/keyword maps to dcterms:subject
Contributor maps to dcterms:contributor
Generic date container maps to dcterms:date
Acceptance date maps exactly to dcterms:dateAccepted
Availability date maps exactly to dcterms:available
Copyright date maps exactly to dcterms:dateCopyrighted
Data collection is a form of creation; closeMatch because collection period may differ from file creation
Temporal or spatial coverage maps to dcterms:coverage
Resource creation date maps exactly to dcterms:created
Issuance date maps exactly to dcterms:issued
Submission date maps exactly to dcterms:dateSubmitted
Update date maps exactly to dcterms:modified
Validity period maps exactly to dcterms:valid
No dcterms equivalent for withdrawal; closest is dcterms:modified; relatedMatch only
Language maps exactly to dcterms:language
Rights statement maps exactly to dcterms:rights
URI of the rights/license maps to dcterms:license
Description maps exactly to dcterms:description
Technical format maps exactly to dcterms:format
Related identifier maps to dcterms:relation; specific relation types map to sub-properties (hasPart, isPartOf, etc.)
Source of derivation maps to dcterms:source
Structured related item maps to dcterms:relation
Alternate identifier is also a dcterms:identifier for the same resource
Version string maps to dcterms:hasVersion
Size/extent maps exactly to dcterms:extent
Spatial location maps to dcterms:spatial (sub-property of dcterms:coverage)
Top-level DataCite resource maps to dcterms:BibliographicResource
Funding reference loosely relates to dcterms:source; no dedicated dcterms class
